data.title: Man Wah Subsidiary RMT Posts Profit Decline but Stronger Balance Sheet in 2025
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Man Wah Holdings ( (HK:1999) ) just unveiled an update.

Man Wah subsidiary Remacro Technology Co., Ltd. has reported a slight decline in revenue and a sharper drop in profitability for 2025, with revenue edging down 2.3% year on year to RMB 2.25 billion and net profit attributable to shareholders falling 17.3% to RMB 186 million. Earnings per share slipped to RMB 0.40, while gross margin and returns on equity narrowed, underscoring pressure on operating performance.

Despite weaker profits, RMT’s balance sheet strengthened over the period, with total assets up 3.3%, net equity attributable to shareholders rising nearly 14%, and gearing ratios improving notably on both parent and consolidated bases. The higher current ratio and interest coverage indicate enhanced solvency and lower leverage, suggesting greater financial resilience even as profit growth slows, which is likely to be closely watched by Man Wah’s investors.

More about Man Wah Holdings

Man Wah Holdings Limited is a Bermuda-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong that operates through various subsidiaries, including Remacro Technology Co., Ltd. RMT is quoted on China’s National Equities Exchange and Quotations System and contributes to the group’s business with its operations in the mainland market.

Average Trading Volume: 5,795,941

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$16.37B

Chongqing Rural Commercial Bank has proposed the nomination of Ms. Cao Shinan and Ms. Gao Yang as independent non-executive directors, following review and approval by its board nomination committee. Chen Lin Education Group Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:1593) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Chen Lin Education Group reported interim unaudited revenue of RMB337.2 million for the six months ended 28 February 2026, essentially flat versus a year earlier. However, gross profit fell to RMB83.5 million and basic earnings per share swung from a profit of RMB0.48 cents to a loss of RMB2.48 cents, reflecting pressure on margins despite stable top-line performance.

The group posted a loss of RMB23.6 million for the period, compared with a profit of RMB4.6 million a year ago, while EBITDA declined from RMB122.1 million to RMB91.6 million. The latest announcement is out from Perfect Medical Health Management Limited ( (HK:1830) ).

Perfect Medical Health Management Limited plans to terminate its existing 2021 share option scheme and replace it with a new 2026 share option scheme aligned with the latest Chapter 17 requirements of Hong Kong’s Listing Rules. Outstanding options totaling 11,304,000 shares will remain valid and exercisable, while the new plan is subject to shareholder approval at an extraordinary general meeting and the Stock Exchange’s listing approval for shares under the new mandate.

Under the proposed 2026 scheme, the board has conditionally granted 50,000,000 share options, representing about 3.98% of issued shares, to Chairman and CEO Dr. Au-Yeung Kong, reinforcing equity-based incentives for the controlling shareholder. This grant will only take effect if shareholders approve the new scheme and independent shareholders endorse the specific award, signaling a governance-sensitive approach to incentivizing top leadership and potentially affecting ownership concentration and long-term alignment with investors.

An update from Beijing Energy International Holding ( (HK:0686) ) is now available.

Beijing Energy International reported that its 271 power plants, including those owned through subsidiaries and associates, generated approximately 6,279,806 MWh of electricity in the first quarter of 2026. The portfolio, spanning 15,981 MW of grid-connected capacity across China, Australia and Vietnam, includes 194 solar, 44 wind, 28 hydro, four energy storage stations and one gas-fired plant.

Subsidiary-owned projects contributed about 5,618,000 MWh, while associates added roughly 661,806 MWh, reflecting broad-based output from both domestic and overseas assets. The figures, drawn from preliminary operating statistics, underscore the group’s growing renewable footprint and provide investors with an early read on operational performance at the start of 2026.

More about Beijing Energy International Holding

Beijing Energy International Holding Co., Ltd. is an independent power producer focused on renewable energy, primarily owning and operating solar, wind and hydro power plants, with additional assets in energy storage and a gas-fired facility. The group’s portfolio is geographically diversified, concentrated across multiple provinces in mainland China and supplemented by projects in Australia and Vietnam, targeting grid-connected clean energy generation.

data.title: Charmacy Pharmaceutical Sets 2026 AGM to Approve Dividend, Credit Line and Share Issue Mandate
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Charmacy Pharmaceutical Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2289) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Charmacy Pharmaceutical Co., Ltd. has called its annual general meeting for 22 May 2026 in Guangzhou, where shareholders will vote on the board and auditor reports and the audited financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025. The meeting will also consider approval of the 2025 profit distribution plan and final dividend, as well as directors’ remuneration for 2026 and the reappointment of Pan-China Certified Public Accountants LLP as auditor.

Shareholders will further be asked to approve a comprehensive revolving credit line of up to RMB2.8 billion for the company and its subsidiaries, authorizing the president to handle related procedures and guarantees. In addition, the board is seeking a general mandate to issue up to 20% of the company’s existing share capital and to amend the articles of association accordingly, a move that could enhance financial flexibility but also has potential dilution implications for existing investors.

More about Charmacy Pharmaceutical Co., Ltd. Class H

Charmacy Pharmaceutical Co., Ltd. is a PRC-incorporated pharmaceutical company listed in Hong Kong, operating in the drug and healthcare distribution sector. The group provides pharmaceutical products and related services in mainland China, positioning itself within the broader Chinese healthcare and pharmaceutical supply chain market.
